content: Microsoft Learn (official, updated 2026-05): SmartScreen checks publisher reputation (signed + trusted cert) and file-hash reputation. Unsigned files always show 'Windows protected your PC' warning requiring More Info > Run Anyway; reputation builds per-file-hash over weeks/hundreds of downloads and does not transfer between versions unless signed with same identity. EV certs NO LONGER bypass SmartScreen (this behavior was removed) — paying for EV solely to avoid SmartScreen is not justified. Microsoft's cheaper 'Artifact Signing' (~/mo, no hardware token, CI/CD-friendly) is the recommended paid option if signing is revisited.
